page: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a 1229 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data places this instance consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1414.2, while multi-core performance reaches 14893.47. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 830.88 GB/sec and an uncached large block read speed of 69.79 GB/sec. In database and application-level tests, the server achieves 13.8 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a PassMark database operations score of 5436.86. For language model tasks, prompt processing with a small 135M model reaches 617.83 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak tier of 24.46 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-16ds_v4 offers a high memory density relative to its compute allocation, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The inclusion of a 1229 GB local disk provides built-in storage capacity without requiring external attachments, though the lack of a GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale AI training. This resource profile is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. The server includes 1229 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results indicate solid mid-tier performance, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 7729.0, a PassMark database score of 5436.86, and Redis SET operations reaching 13.8 million operations per second. While it exhibits average performance in memory bandwidth and text processing, its medium-model LLM prompt processing is relatively weak at 24.46 tokens per second. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v4 is an EDSv4-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: An Intel-powered virtual machine offering high memory density and local storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
